title: lora_l2.c: init idempotence check has TOCTOU race
description:
In lichen_lora_l2_init() (lora_l2.c:145-147), the idempotence check uses atomic_get() without CAS, so two concurrent calls could both see initialized==0 and proceed to reinitialize.

What goes wrong: If two threads call init() concurrently (e.g., NET_DEVICE_INIT + main.c both triggering), the EUI-64 could be regenerated mid-use by another thread, causing address inconsistency. The code comment says 'prevents EUI-64 regeneration on double-init' but the implementation doesn't guarantee it.

The fix is straightforward but this review is read-only: use atomic_cas() or hold a mutex for the initialization sequence.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:145-147
close_reason: Fix verified: mutex acquired at line 163 before checking initialized at line 170, preventing TOCTOU race. Security comment at lines 168-169 documents the fix.
